task_yaml.task:
We're about to import a legacy order dump into the new system and I need
to know what's broken before it goes in. The SQLite dump lives at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/orders.db.sql — load it up with the
SQL toolkit skill or the database operations skill, poke around, and flag the
obvious data-quality problems that would make this risky to import:
negative unit prices, duplicated business identifiers, broken
relationships between tables, and rows that fall outside the original
December 2010 import window.

Drop the findings in /tmp_workspace/results/anomalies.json, grouped by
kind of problem, with enough detail that I can pull up the offending
rows myself. Don't over-report — only rows that actually violate the
rule.

Also save:
- /tmp_workspace/results/anomaly_rows.csv with one row per offending row
  and columns anomaly_type, table_name, primary_key, order_number,
  customer_id, product_id, evidence_value, lookup_sql.
  If one row violates more than one rule, include one CSV row per rule so
  each reason is visible.
- /tmp_workspace/results/reproduce.sql with the exact SELECT statements
  you used for each issue type, so I can rerun the checks.
